pi_items.source: Task description specifies this exact path
Task description specifies this exact path
Matches Bridge Command's Scenarios directory structure as documented in GitHub repository and install scripts

Task description explicitly specifies this header format. However, Bridge Command's default boat.ini files do not contain MaxSpeed, Length, or Beam parameters - only Depth (mapped to Draft). The export_result.sh script will extract '0' or 'N/A' for missing fields.

GitHub bridgecommand/bc repository contains: 14 Ownship models (3111_Tug, Alice Upjohn, Alkmini, Aquarius_Tug, Atlantic85, CSL_Wattle, HMAS_Westralia, Protis, ProtisSingleScrew, Puffer, ShetlandTrader, VIC56, VIC56_360, Waverley) and 39 Othership models (3111_Tug, Alkmini, CSL_Wattle, Cargoship1, Cargoship2, CohoFerry, CohoFerry_Sinking, HMAS_Armidale, HMAS_Brunei, HMAS_Huon, HMAS_Perth, HMAS_Waller, HMAS_Westralia, HMS_Daring, HURRICANE_RHIB, Jim_Bickhoff, Justin, LifeRaft, ManOverboard, Namoh, Protis, Puffer, RV_Triton, ShetlandTrader, ShipsLifeboat, SmallTrawler, SmallTrawlerNets, SmallTrawler_Sinking, Smit_Rotterdam, Timbercarrier, VIC56, Waverley, Yacht, Yacht_Motoring, Yacht_Port, Yacht_Sinking, Yacht_Stbd, nouveau, p2000). Additionally: Buoy, LandObject, ManOverboard object types.
Bridge Command provides 39 Othership models available for traffic vessels, exceeding the minimum of 5 different vessel types required

pi_items.metadata_value: Each subdirectory of /opt/bridgecommand/Models/ represents a ship model
pi_items.verified_value: Bridge Command's actual structure has Models/Ownship/ and Models/Othership/ subdirectories, which in turn contain individual ship models. The default structure is not flat with individual models directly under Models/.
pi_items.source: GitHub bridgecommand/bc repository structure: https://github.com/bridgecommand/bc/tree/main/bin/Models shows Ownship, Othership, Buoy, LandObject, ManOverboard as subdirectories

pi_items.metadata_value: boat.ini files contain parameters: MaxSpeed, Length, Beam, Draft as specified in CSV header
pi_items.verified_value: Bridge Command boat.ini files contain: Depth, Mass, MaxRevs, Various propulsion/dynamics parameters. They do NOT contain: MaxSpeed, Length, Beam/Width, or Name parameters
pi_items.source: Examined actual boat.ini files from GitHub: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bridgecommand/bc/main/bin/Models/Ownship/Protis/boat.ini and https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bridgecommand/bc/main/bin/Models/Ownship/Alice%20Upjohn/boat.ini. Export script will return '0' for missing numeric fields.

privileged_info_summary: The fleet_technical_manual task uses Bridge Command, an open-source maritime ship simulator available at https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk (GitHub: bridgecommand/bc). The environment builds Bridge Command from source and includes 14 default Ownship models and 39 Othership models, totaling 53+ catalogable models, exceeding the minimum requirement of 8. Output files are correctly specified as /home/ga/Documents/fleet_technical_manual.txt and /home/ga/Documents/model_index.csv, with the scenario placed at /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/z) Fleet Review. However, two structural issues exist: (1) Bridge Command's actual directory structure has Models/Ownship/ and Models/Othership/ subdirectories containing individual models, not a flat structure, and (2) the required CSV header includes MaxSpeed, Length, and Beam fields, but Bridge Command's default boat.ini configuration files do not contain these parameters—only Depth is available. The export_result.sh script will extract '0' or 'N/A' for these missing fields.
